{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about local anesthetics (LAs) acting on nerve membranes. From the provided documents, we learn that LAs block voltage-gated sodium channels, which are responsible for the influx of sodium ions during the depolarization phase of the action potential. This blockage prevents the generation of action potentials, thereby numbing the area. The options mention either sodium (Na+) or potassium (K+) ions moving from the interior to the exterior or from the exterior to the interior of the cell. Since LAs block the influx of sodium ions from the exterior to the interior, the correct answer is B.", "answer_choice": "B"}
